name: Cherry Cobbler Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Grease an 11 x 7-inch pan.
3. Mix flour and baking powder in a bowl.
4. In a large bowl, beat butter and powdered sugar with an electric mixer until pale and fluffy.
5. Beat in egg and lemon peel.
6. Gradually add the flour mixture to the butter mixture on low speed.
7. Remove 2/3 cup of dough and set aside.
8. Use remaining dough to pat over the bottom of the prepared pan.
9. Spread cherry pie filling evenly over the dough, reaching the edges of the pan.
10. Roll the reserved dough into 1-inch balls and flatten them slightly.
11. Place the flattened dough balls evenly over the cherry pie filling.
12. Bake for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Makes 18 bars.
Apple Kuchen variation:
1. Drain 20 ounces of apple pie slices and mix with 1/2 cup golden raisins and 1 teaspoon cinnamon.
2. Spread the mixture evenly over the dough using a spatula, breaking up apples if necessary to cover the dough completely.
3. Roll the remaining dough into long ropes and lay them diagonally over the apple slices to create a criss-cross effect.
4. Proceed with baking as directed.
